title: 10 Enterprise Fraud Management Reports
body_text:
10	Enterprise Fraud Management Reports
10.1	Introduction to Enterprise Fraud Management Reports
Enterprise Fraud Management generates multiple sets of reports related to alerted transactions, triggered rules, and model summaries. These reports track performance across specified time ranges and provide overviews of various analytic measures. Access to the reports is limited by your role. See Section 4.2, EFM User Roles, for more information.
Note:
Training videos
are available for the procedures in this chapter.
10.2	Accessing Enterprise Fraud Management Reports
To access the Reports:
Log in to EFM as described in Section 5.2, Logging in to Enterprise Fraud Management.
Select
Reports
on the Dashboard page.
Select the applicable Management Information category from the left-hand navigation. The reports in the main body of the page defaults to the Alerts Count & Value Management Information page. See Figure 1.
ГўВ‘
Alerts Count & Value
ГўВЂВ“ Tracks alert counts or values and separates the total amounts of the alerts in reports by date, event type, and review status.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Alerts Generated
ГўВЂВ“ Tracks generated alerts and separates the amounts of the alerts into reports by date, event type, and review status.
ГўВ‘Вў
Alerts Closed
ГўВЂВ“ Tracks alerts closed by their close date, event types, and review status.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Rules Summary
ГўВЂВ“ Tracks the number of rules triggered over a period of time and are grouped by rule.
ГўВ‘В¤
Model Summary
ГўВЂВ“
Tracks model and rule performance over time. It also provides overviews of analytic reporting (e.g., values, volumes, and rates) across various analytic measures.
ГўВ‘ВҐ
TP/FP Over Time
ГўВЂВ“ These are the True Positive / False Positive Over Time reports. It provides a time summary by model reports and summarizes analytics reports. The reports provide values, volumes, and rates within a specified amount of time across various analytic measurements.
Figure 1: Management Information Categories
10.3	Using Filters on Reports
Once you select a Management Information category, you can use filters to refine data displayed in the report. This section describes the filters available and how to use filters on EFM generated reports. However, the filters available in each category differ.
The following options allow you to filter reports:
Alert Source
ГўВЂВ“ Filters data by alert activity, such as when alerts are created manually or by a model or rule. This filter is limited to alert-related reports.
Entity Type
ГўВЂВ“ Shows data related to the customer of a transaction. This filter is limited to Rules Summary reports.
Event Type
ГўВЂВ“ Shows activity related to paymentRT, paymentNRT, or wireRT events.
Investigator
ГўВЂВ“ Filters alert activity related to a specific investigator. This filter is limited to alert-related reports.
Label Type
ГўВЂВ“ Filters data related to alerted transactions identified as Risk or No-risk. This filter is limited to Model Summary and TP/FP Over Time reports.
Model
ГўВЂВ“ Filters activity related to specific rules or a type of model. This filter is limited to the Model Summary reports.
Reporting Value
ГўВЂВ“ Filters data by event attributes. This filter is limited to Model Summary and TP/FP Over Time reports.
Review Status
ГўВЂВ“ Filters data by alerts identified as risk or alerts that are unreviewed. This filter is limited to alert-related reports.
Rules
ГўВЂВ“ Shows activity related to one or more selected rules. This filter is limited to the Rules Summary reports.
Solution/Tenant
ГўВЂВ“ This filter only applies to those with landlord roles. This filter is limited to the Rules Summary, Model Summary, and TP/FP Over Time reports.
Status
ГўВЂВ“ Shows activity related to active or closed alerts. This filter is limited to alert-related reports.
Threshold
ГўВЂВ“ Shows data via the value returned by a model ranging from 0 (no-risk) to 1 (highest risk). This option returns all values above the threshold. This filter is limited to the Model Summary and TP/FP Over Time reports.
Time
ГўВЂВ“ Shows any activity generated between selected dates or times and specific time intervals.
Creating a Time Filter
The Time filter is available in all Management Information categories. Once you select a Management Information category, navigate to the right-hand side of the page to create a Time Filter:
ГўВ‘
Select the date and time in the Time Range field. See Figure 2.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the Range Type from its drop-down menu.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select a custom time range in the Start (Inclusive) fields. Depending on what custom time range you select, you may need to enter relative or specific dates and times.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select a custom time range in the End (Exclusive) field(s). Depending on what custom time range you select, you may need to enter relative or specific dates and times.
Important
:	Reporting data is only available starting from November 5, 2023. Reporting data is limited to the UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) time zone. Anytime a time range filter is used, convert the date(s) and time(s) of the filter selections to UTC. The UTC time zone cannot be modified.
ГўВ‘В¤
Select
Apply
to confirm your selections and to update the reports.
ГўВ‘ВҐ
Alternatively, select a time interval from the Time Grain field to create a different time filter. The Time Grain Filter is automatically applied to the reports after a selection is made.
Figure 2: Creating a Time Filter
Creating Other Filters
Navigate to the right-hand side of the page below the Time Filter. To create a filter other than the Time Filter:
ГўВ‘
Select any option from a filter drop-down menu. See Figure 3.
Note:	Multiple options from a single filter can be applied. Repeat Step 1 as needed. The fields displayed in the image will vary depending on the filter type.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Alternatively, to remove a selected filter or a filter option, select
x
.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select
Apply
to confirm your selection(s) and to update the reports. The page will display the updated report information.
Figure 3: Creating a Filter
To clear all time filters and filters:
Navigate to and select the specific Management Information category to refresh all reports and remove all applied filters.
10.4	Viewing and Downloading Reports
EFM provides various methods to support the viewing experience of their reports. When viewing reports, you can perform the following:
View additional alert-related reports (only available in alert-related Management Information categories)
View filters that have and havenГўВЂВ™t been applied
Refresh a reportГўВЂВ™s data
Adjust the visual size of a report
Download or export reports
View graphical reports with interactive tools
Many reports are automatically displayed when you select a Management Information category. Additional reports are limited to alert-related Management Information categories.
To view additional alert-related reports:
Navigate to and select any alert-related Management Information category (e.g., Alerts Count & Value, Alerts Generated, or Alerts Closed).
Select a report type displayed above the body of the page. See Figure 4. The selected report is underlined in dark blue.
Figure 4: Viewing Additional Alert-Related Reports
To view applied or unset filters for a selected report:
Select the Applied Filters icon. The list of applied or unset filters is expanded by selecting the arrow icon. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Selecting the Applied Filters Icon
To refresh a reportГўВЂВ™s data, increase the visual size of a report, and download or export a report:
Select the kebab menu beside the selected report, then select one of the following options (see Figure 6):
ГўВ‘
Force refresh
ГўВЂВ“ Refreshes the selected report.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Maximize chart
ГўВЂВ“ Increases the visual size of the selected report.
ГўВ‘Вў
Download as image
ГўВЂВ“ Allows you to download only a JPEG image of the report.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Export CSV
ГўВЂВ“ Allows you to export the report in CSV format only.
Figure 6: Selecting the Kebab Menu
All Management Information pages generate graphical reports with their own set of interactive tools. The interactive tools allow you to adjust the view of data, select and create data visualizations, and adjust the scale of values used in the report.
To use an interactive tool, select a tool provided in the graphical report. The Zoom, Box Select, Lasso Select, Horizontally Select, and Vertically Select tools require you to interact with the graphical report after its respective tool is selected. See Figure 7.
Interactive tool options include:
ГўВ‘
Restore
ГўВЂВ“ Resets and clears all interactive tool activity from the chart.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Zoom
ГўВЂВ“ Select a desired area on the graphical report that you wish to see in more detail.
ГўВ‘Вў
Zoom Reset
ГўВЂВ“ Resets any zoomed in areas to the default view.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Display
ГўВЂВ“ Change the type of graph used for the report.
ГўВ‘В¤
Value
ГўВЂВ“ Increase or decrease the scale of selected values used for the report.
ГўВ‘ВҐ
Box Select
ГўВЂВ“ Select a portion of graph data in box format.
ГўВ‘В¦
Lasso Select
ГўВЂВ“ Select a portion of graph data in a free-form format.
ГўВ‘В§
Horizontally Select
ГўВЂВ“ Select a portion of graph data horizontally.
ГўВ‘ВЁ
Vertically Select
ГўВЂВ“ Select a portion of graph data vertically.
ГўВ‘В©
Keep Selections
ГўВЂВ“ Allows you to keep two or more selected portions visible on the graph when selected.
ГўВ‘ВЄ
Clear Selections
ГўВЂВ“ Clears all selection activity.
Figure 7: Using a Graphical ReportГўВЂВ™s Interactive Tools
END
